content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and Chefina has an integer sequence B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​. Since Chef is a genius, he always keeps himself busy with some tasks. One day, Chef took the sequences AAA and BBB and constructed a matrix CCC with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM) such that for each valid iii and jjj, the value in row iii and column jjj is Ci,j=Ai⊕BjC_{i,j} = A_i \oplus B_jCi,j​=Ai​⊕Bj​, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operator.

Chefina is curious about this matrix, but she does not have a lot of time, so she asked Chef to tell her some information about it. For each integer LLL from 111 to max(N,M)\mathrm{max}(N, M)max(N,M) inclusive:


For all integers XXX and YYY such that 1≤X,Y≤L1 \le X, Y \le L1≤X,Y≤L, consider all submatrices of CCC with XXX rows and YYY columns.
The beauty of a submatrix is defined as its size (the number of elements in it) multiplied by the XOR of all elements in the submatrix.
Chef should find the sum of beauties of all these submatrices modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
**Note:** The definition of the submatrix is used according to here.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 65,00065,00065,000
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 65,00065,00065,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing max(N,M)\mathrm{max}(N, M)max(N,M) space-separated integers ― the sums of beauties for L=1,2,…,max(N,M)L = 1, 2, \ldots, \mathrm{max}(N, M)L=1,2,…,max(N,M), modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
